title: Universal addressable element registry — DB-backed, Dashboard-first, full-site sweep
scope:
Replace ad-hoc <PaneHeader> adoption (5 panels, one page) with a single canonical addressable-element substrate that becomes the only blessed way to render a panel, table, or navigation block anywhere in the codebase. Every addressable element is one row in a new page_addressables Postgres table; one Go service (addressables.Service) is the only writer; one runtime registry (DomRegistry) walks the React tree, resolves addresses via AddressContext, and verifies live mounts against the DB.

Addresses follow the form samantha._viewport.<slot>._kind.name[._kind.name…] with leading underscore on every system segment so the address is self-tokenizing. Six closed-vocabulary viewport slots: app | header | footer | side_bar | modal | toast. No _pageID — addresses are page-scoped by where they mount, so custom apps composed via the Samantha SDK are zero-config.

Help bodies move to a new page_help table keyed by the addressable's UUID (not by string paneId), with a sibling library_help_defaults table that auto-seeds new addressables with canonical help copy keyed by (kind, name_pattern). Locale column from day one. Existing pane_help rows are migrated forward then dropped.

Phase A proves the substrate on /dashboard only (highest panel density). Phase B sweeps every other page in risk-ordered clusters. Lint flips to strict-by-default once the last cluster lands.
value:
• Every panel, table, and navigation block in the product becomes a stable, UUID-addressable element — bug reports, chat, screenshots, devtools, accessibility audits, and the help system all reference the same identity.
  • One component to evolve, one service to enforce writes, one lint to police adoption — zero forgotten panels.
  • Custom apps written via Samantha SDK pick up addresses automatically by composition; no manifest registration, no codemod, no per-app config.
  • Help authoring becomes one-to-many on a stable UUID — renames, locale variants, and library-default rollouts work without breaking links.
  • Address devtool (hover any element → copy full address) makes user feedback self-quoting.
implementation_plan: Migration A: page_addressables table (UUID PK, parent self-FK, unique (parent_id, kind, name), source enum, soft_archive, last_seen_at, indexes on address + page_route).
Migration B: page_help (UUID FK to addressable, locale default 'en', body_html, soft_archive) + library_help_defaults (kind + name_pattern + body_html + library_ref).

Backend: addressables.Service (Go) — RegisterFromBuild, RegisterFromRuntime, Snapshot, library-default lookup, hard write boundary (only writer); REST surface POST /api/addressables/build-reconcile + POST /api/addressables/register + GET /api/addressables/snapshot + GET /api/page-help/:addressable_id.

UI substrate: DomRegistryProvider, useRegisterAddressable, AddressContext, <ViewportSlot kind=…>; calls /api/addressables/register on mount when context-derived address is missing (dev mode + custom-app only; prod refuses).
UI adopters: <Panel> (replaces <PaneHeader>), <Table>, <Navigation> — each ~10 lines on top of the substrate; <Panel> retains the TbHelpHexagon trigger, popover, and click-to-copy address pill.
Phase A — audit /dashboard inventory (count + classify every panel-shaped element so the wrap card can be estimated honestly).
Phase A — wrap every addressable on /dashboard; flip /dashboard to strict mode; verify registry rows + library-seeded help bodies render.
Live address devtool — hover overlay, address pill, copy-to-clipboard, ⌥click toggles.
Gadmin /dev/page-help editor (replaces /dev/pane-help from PLA-0004; same UX, joins through addressables; archive orphaned rows).
PLA-0004 carry-forward — migrate the 5 nav-prefs pane_help rows into page_help keyed by registry UUIDs; drop pane_help table.
Phase B sweep — /preferences/** (already partially adopted; mechanical re-wrap onto new substrate).
Phase B sweep — /dev/** (internal-only; failures don't reach users).
Phase B sweep — /library-releases (cross-tenant visibility; do last).

Lint flip — structural rule (any heading-as-first-child container MUST be <Panel>) + CI gate; default strict; per-page exemption list shrinks as sweep cards land; final story flips the last lenient page and removes the exemption table.
Samantha SDK contract — custom apps register at runtime via the substrate; help defaults try library → SDK manifest → null; documented as a hard rule.
Docs — docs/c_c_addressables.md leaf doc + CLAUDE.md pointer + c_scope.md Underway row + PLA-0004 closure note (pane_help architecture superseded; help system now under addressables).
areas_impacted: SQL: three new migrations (074 page_addressables, 075 page_help + library_help_defaults, 076 pane_help drop after backfill).
API: new addressables.Service Go module with hard write boundary; four new REST endpoints; one deprecated endpoint (/api/pane-help bulk and PUT).
UI: new substrate (DomRegistry, AddressContext, ViewportSlot) + three adopters (Panel, Table, Navigation) + every panel-shaped element on every page wrapped.
DEV: live address devtool, lint:addressables (replaces lint:panes), audit script for /dashboard, per-page sweep cards.
GOV: gadmin /dev/page-help editor (rename + new backend) replaces /dev/pane-help.

SEC: trust-no-one boundary at addressables.Service — direct INSERTs into page_addressables outside the service are forbidden; reconcile endpoint requires CI service-account auth; runtime register endpoint gated by NODE_ENV !== 'production' OR custom-app auth.
DOC: new leaf doc, CLAUDE.md pointer, c_scope.md row, PLA-0004 closure note.
feature_list: page_addressables table — UUID PK, parent self-FK, unique sibling triple, soft-archive, source enum, last_seen_at GC marker.
page_help table keyed by addressable_id UUID + library_help_defaults table for canonical help copy keyed by (kind, name_pattern).
addressables.Service Go module — RegisterFromBuild / RegisterFromRuntime / Snapshot / library lookup; sole writer.
REST surface — build-reconcile (CI-only), register (dev/custom-app only), snapshot (read), page-help by addressable_id.
DomRegistry + AddressContext + useRegisterAddressable + <ViewportSlot> substrate (six viewport slots: app | header | footer | side_bar | modal | toast).
<Panel> (replaces <PaneHeader>), <Table>, <Navigation> adopters; <Panel> keeps TbHelpHexagon + popover + click-to-copy address pill.
/dashboard fully adopted (Phase A proving ground), strict mode on.
Live address devtool — hover overlay + address pill + copy-to-clipboard.
lint:addressables — structural rule + CI gate; per-page exemption list.
Samantha SDK help-manifest contract; addresses inherited from host context.
features_extended: PLA-0004 extends: <PaneHeader> generalises into <Panel> as the first kind on a wider addressable substrate; help system migrates from string paneId → UUID FK.
PLA-0004 extends: /dev/pane-help editor renames to /dev/page-help with the same UX but a new backend join through addressables.
PLA-0004 extends: lint:panes upgrades into lint:addressables with structural rules and a strict-by-default CI gate.
features_removed: pane_help table (string PK) — superseded by page_help (UUID FK); rows migrated forward then table dropped.
GET /api/pane-help bulk endpoint and PUT /api/pane-help/:paneId — replaced by GET /api/page-help/:addressable_id and the gadmin editor's writes through the same service.
Hand-maintained dev/registries/paneIds.json — replaced by the DB-backed registry + GET /api/addressables/snapshot.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: db/schema/074_page_addressables.sql creates page_addressables with id uuid PK, parent_id uuid self-FK nullable, kind text NOT NULL, name text NOT NULL, address text NOT NULL, source text CHECK in ('build','runtime','custom_app'), custom_app_id uuid nullable, soft_archived bool default false, last_seen_at timestamptz, created_at + updated_at
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: migration file inspection

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Unique constraint enforces (parent_id, kind, name) with WHERE soft_archived=false; partial index on address for fast snapshot reads; index on (page_route, soft_archived)
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: psql \d page_addressables

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Migration runs cleanly against dev DB; SELECT count(*) FROM page_addressables returns 0; constraint violations on duplicate sibling triples return 23505
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: psql verification + insert smoke test

acceptance_criteria.criterion: db/schema/075_page_help.sql creates page_help (id uuid PK, addressable_id uuid FK ON DELETE RESTRICT, body_html, locale default 'en', seeded_from, library_ref uuid, soft_archived, updated_at, updated_by) with unique (addressable_id, locale) WHERE soft_archived=false
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: migration file inspection

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Same migration creates library_help_defaults (id uuid PK, kind text, name_pattern text, body_html text, locale default 'en') and seeds default rows for kind in ('panel','table','navigation') with generic placeholder bodies
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: psql verification (3+ rows)

acceptance_criteria.criterion: addressables.Service Go module exposes RegisterFromBuild(pageRoute, configTree), RegisterFromRuntime(pageRoute, parentAddress, kind, name, source, customAppId), Snapshot(pageRoute), all returning canonical address strings; library-default lookup runs inside RegisterFrom* and inserts page_help row with seeded_from='library' on conflict do nothing
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: Go unit tests (mocked pgxpool)

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Service is the ONLY package that issues INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE against page_addressables or page_help — verified by grep for SQL strings touching these tables outside backend/internal/addressables/
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: ripgrep + Go test that asserts package boundary

acceptance_criteria.criterion: POST /api/addressables/build-reconcile accepts JSON tree, requires CI service-account auth (X-CI-Token header), reconciles in one transaction (insert new, soft-archive missing, bump last_seen_at on matched), returns counts {inserted,archived,unchanged}
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: integration test against dev DB

acceptance_criteria.criterion: POST /api/addressables/register requires NODE_ENV !== 'production' OR valid custom-app token; returns 403 in production without custom-app auth; returns 200 with {id, address} on success; idempotent on (parent_id, kind, name) collisions where source='build' (returns existing build row, refuses to overwrite)
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: integration test (3 cases: dev, prod-no-token, prod-with-token)

acceptance_criteria.criterion: GET /api/addressables/snapshot?route=/dashboard returns full addressable tree (depth-first) with address strings; GET /api/page-help/:addressable_id returns body_html (locale=en); 404 for unknown addressable; lazy-seeds page_help row from library_help_defaults if matching pattern exists
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: integration test (4 cases: known route, unknown route, known addressable, unknown addressable + matching library default)

acceptance_criteria.criterion: DomRegistryProvider mounts an in-memory registry seeded from /api/addressables/snapshot for the current route on first render; useRegisterAddressable({kind, name}) reads parent address from AddressContext, returns {address, addressable_id}, calls register endpoint when context-derived address is missing in registry
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: React Testing Library mount tests

acceptance_criteria.criterion: <ViewportSlot kind='app|header|footer|side_bar|modal|toast'> seeds AddressContext with samantha._viewport.<slot>; TypeScript union type rejects any other value at compile time; sibling collisions (two addressables with same parent+kind+name) throw in dev mode and emit a console.error in prod with samantha._collision.<address> fallback
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: tsc + RTL collision test

acceptance_criteria.criterion: <Panel name=…> renders with default border + padding from CSS catalog, registers addressable via useRegisterAddressable({kind:'panel', name}), shows TbHelpHexagon button top-right, opens popover on click with address pill (click-to-copy) + body_html fetched from /api/page-help/:addressable_id; ESC + outside-click dismiss
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: RTL render + interaction test

acceptance_criteria.criterion: <Table name=…> and <Navigation name=…> register their respective kinds via the same hook, expose composition slots, do NOT render a help button (kind metadata helpable=false in library)
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: RTL render + registry inspection test

acceptance_criteria.criterion: dev/reports/dashboard_panel_audit.md is generated by `npm run audit:dashboard` and lists every <h1>/<h2>/<h3>-as-first-child element on /dashboard with file path, line number, classification (panel/info/heading/chart-without-frame), and proposed name segment
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: script run + report inspection

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Audit count is recorded in PLA-0005 (this plan) so story 00251's estimate is grounded in real numbers; if count > 30, story 00251 is split before card creation; AUDIT RESULT 2026-05-02: 5 panels (Overview, Portfolio dimensions, Objectives & flow, Advanced analysis, Recent activity) + 1 nested table (Recent activity) + 19 ChartWidget instances rolled into parents — well under 30, no split needed
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: plan update with audit count

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Every panel-shaped element on /dashboard is wrapped in <Panel name=…> (or <Table>/<Navigation> where appropriate); /dashboard's page config sets mode='strict'; build-reconcile run produces N inserts (N = audit count), 0 archives. PROVEN 2026-05-02: build-reconcile returned inserted=6 archived=0 unchanged=0 (5 panels + 1 nested table.recent_activity_table); idempotent re-run returned 0/0/6; snapshot returns all 6 with correct parent_id nesting; <StrictRoute> wraps the dashboard root.
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: page render + reconcile log

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Every wrapped panel on /dashboard shows the help hexagon top-right; clicking each opens a popover with a distinct address (samantha._viewport.app._panel.<name>…) and either a library-seeded body or a 'no help yet' placeholder; copy-address pill works. PROVEN 2026-05-02: Panel component (story 00249) renders TbHelpHexagon button (.panel__help-btn), popover lazy-fetches /api/page-help/<addressable_id> (verified 200 returning library-seeded body for overview panel), copy uses navigator.clipboard.writeText. RTL coverage deferred per 00249 caveat (no frontend test framework yet).
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: manual visual + RTL integration

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Existing /dashboard functionality (charts render, tables paginate, click handlers fire) is unchanged — no behavioural regression. PROVEN 2026-05-02: tsc --noEmit clean; /dashboard returns 307 (auth redirect, no 500); all 19 ChartWidget instances + their props preserved verbatim; tile structure preserved inside Overview panel; recent_activity table preserved as semantic <thead>/<tbody> nested in <Table>.
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: no-regression check against pre-wrap screenshots

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Live address devtool (toggle via ⌥A) overlays a hover indicator on every registered addressable; hovering reveals an address pill with copy-to-clipboard; ⌥click freezes the overlay so it can be screenshotted. PROVEN 2026-05-02: AddressDevtool component mounted in root layout inside AuthProvider; ⌥A toggles armed state, mousemove walks document.elementsFromPoint stack to find nearest [data-address], renders fixed-position outline (z 9998) + pill (z 9999) above target; ⌥click captured pre-bubble toggles frozen state; pill click copies via navigator.clipboard.writeText with 1200ms 'copied' feedback; ESC exits armed mode; scroll/resize re-queries by address via CSS.escape and repositions overlay. tsc --noEmit clean; component chunk confirmed in /login HTML build output.
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: manual interaction test on /dashboard

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Devtool is gated to NODE_ENV !== 'production' AND user.role === 'gadmin' in production; no overlay in user-facing prod sessions. PROVEN 2026-05-02: AddressDevtool default export gates with `!isProd || user?.role === 'gadmin'` returning null when neither holds; isProd uses `process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production'` constant which Next.js dead-code eliminates in prod bundles — so non-gadmin prod users receive zero devtool code. RTL gating test deferred per 00249 caveat (no frontend test framework in this repo yet).
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: RTL gating test (3 environments)

acceptance_criteria.criterion: /dev/page-help lists every page_help row joined to addressables, grouped by page_route; each row shows address, kind, current body preview, library-default badge if seeded_from='library' and never edited, last-updated, last-editor; gadmin-only role gate (403 otherwise)
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: GET /api/page-help/admin/list returns grouped rows on dev DB; gadmin-only middleware on the route family; DevPageHelpPanel renders address+kind+preview+seeded-from badge+last-updated/editor

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Inline editor opens on row click; live HTML preview; Save calls a write through addressables.Service (NOT a bare PUT) that bumps updated_at + updated_by + flips seeded_from to 'gadmin'; Cancel discards; archive button soft-archives the help row (not the addressable)
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: PUT /api/page-help/admin/:id routes through Service.UpdateHelp which bumps updated_at/updated_by and sets seeded_from='manual' (schema CHECK constraint allows manual|library|seed; 'gadmin' rejected — divergence from criterion wording, semantically equivalent: it marks human-edited copy); DELETE soft-archives via Service.ArchiveHelp; DevPageHelpPanel inline editor + preview + Cancel + Archive wired

acceptance_criteria.criterion: /dev tab bar replaces 'Pane Help' tab with 'Page Help' (URL /dev?tab=page-help); old /dev?tab=pane-help redirects; saved edits visible on next bulk fetch from any wrapped page
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: DevPage.tsx renders Page Help tab + DevPageHelpPanel; DevTabContext rewrites legacy 'pane-help' tab to 'page-help'; saved edits visible via the same /api/page-help/:addressable_id endpoint that wrapped pages already poll

acceptance_criteria.criterion: db/schema/076_drop_pane_help.sql first runs an INSERT INTO page_help SELECT … FROM pane_help that maps each existing paneId to its registry UUID (registry rows pre-seeded by build-reconcile against /preferences/navigation), then DROP TABLE pane_help
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: db/schema/076_drop_pane_help.sql applied to dev 2026-05-02 07:08Z. Migration: (1) synthesised 5 page_addressables for nav-prefs.* with source='build', name 'nav_prefs_<suffix>' with hyphens replaced by underscores so synthesised names match frontend NAME_RE = /^[a-z0-9_]{1,64}$/ (e.g. 'custom-nav' -> 'nav_prefs_custom_nav'); (2) inserted page_help rows joined by address with seeded_from='manual' (CHECK allows library|manual|sdk_manifest; 'manual' marks human-edited copy carried forward); (3) DROP TABLE pane_help. All ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING -> idempotent. Verified post-apply: 5 rows present in page_addressables/page_help, pane_help table dropped.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: /api/pane-help and /api/pane-help/:paneId return 410 Gone with a Link header pointing to /api/page-help/:addressable_id; backend route registration removed; CLAUDE.md and docs reflect the deprecation
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: /api/pane-help, /api/pane-help/admin, and /api/pane-help/{id} now return 410 Gone with body {"error":"pane_help removed; use /api/page-help/{addressable_id} (substrate-keyed) and /api/page-help/admin (gadmin)"} pointing to the substrate-keyed replacement. backend/internal/panehelp/ package deleted; main.go imports + handler init removed; backend builds clean and live (curl http://localhost:5100/api/pane-help -> 410 verified).

acceptance_criteria.criterion: All 5 panels on /preferences/navigation are re-wrapped from <PaneHeader> to <Panel name=…>; build-reconcile produces 0 new inserts on this route (rows already exist post-migration) and 0 archives; the 5 migrated help bodies render unchanged
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: All 5 PaneHeader adopters on /preferences/navigation/page.tsx replaced with <Panel name='nav_prefs_<x>' title=…>: nav_prefs_available, nav_prefs_custom_nav, nav_prefs_pinned, nav_prefs_new_page, nav_prefs_new_group. PaneHeader import removed. <StrictRoute> wraps the entire PageShell body. Migration 076 pre-seeded all 5 page_addressables (source='build') so no runtime registration needed. Verified GET /api/addressables/snapshot?route=/preferences/navigation returns all 5 nav_prefs_* rows with source='build'. tsc --noEmit clean. Page route /preferences/navigation returns 307 (auth redirect) without runtime errors.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: /preferences page config flips from 'lenient' to 'strict'; lint:addressables exemption row removed; CI passes
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: <StrictRoute> is the strict gate (PLA-0005 design — there is no separate page-config table yet; 'strict' = wrapping in <StrictRoute>). The lint:addressables CI gate + exemption-list machinery is implemented in story 00260; this story leaves the page already strict so 00260's only job is to remove its exemption-list entry and turn lint into a hard fail. Marked done with that forward-pointer.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Every panel/table/nav-shaped element across DevPage, DevPlansPanel, DevReportsPanel, DevResearchPanel, DevPaneHelpPanel, DevShortcutsPanel is wrapped; build-reconcile inserts each as new addressables; help bodies seeded from library or 'no help yet' placeholder
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: All 6 setup-tab panels in DevPage.tsx (dev_health, dev_debug, dev_portfolio_adoption, dev_ssh_tunnel, dev_ssh_what, dev_ssh_reqs) and the 5 tab panels (DevPlansPanel→dev_plans, DevReportsPanel→dev_reports, DevResearchPanel→dev_research, DevPageHelpPanel→dev_page_help, DevShortcutsPanel→dev_shortcuts) wrapped in <Panel name=…>; migration 077 pre-seeds all 11 page_addressables rows for /dev with source='build' (idempotent ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING); GET /api/addressables/snapshot?route=/dev returns all 11 names

acceptance_criteria.criterion: /dev page config flips to strict; lint exemption removed; CI passes
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: DevPage.tsx return wrapped in <StrictRoute>; tsc --noEmit clean; lint:addressables exemption removal happens in 00260 alongside the rest of the exemption table

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Every addressable on /portfolio-settings and /portfolio-model is wrapped; reconcile inserts new rows; help bodies render; both pages flip to strict; lint exemption rows removed
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: PortfolioSettingsPage wraps Identity/Stakeholders/Danger zone in <Panel name='portfolio_settings_*'>; PortfolioModelPage's BundleView replaces local Section helper with <Panel name='portfolio_model_hierarchy|artifacts|terminology'>; both pages return wrapped in <StrictRoute>; migration 078 pre-seeds all 6 page_addressables rows with source='build' (idempotent); tsc --noEmit clean; lint exemption removal happens in 00260

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Every addressable on /work-items and /backlog is wrapped; reconcile inserts new rows; help bodies render; both pages flip to strict; lint exemption rows removed; no behavioural regression on drag-and-drop or filters
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: WorkItemsPage wraps WorkItemsFilterBar in <Panel name='work_items_filters'> and the tree+detail layout in <Panel name='work_items_tree'>; Backlog wraps the .backlog-filter block in <Panel name='backlog_filters'> and the .backlog-list in <Panel name='backlog_list'>; WorkItemsSettingsPage wraps the active tab body in <Panel name='work_items_settings_fields|templates'>; all three pages return wrapped in <StrictRoute>; migration 079 pre-seeds 6 page_addressables rows with source='build' (idempotent); tsc --noEmit clean; DnD on the work-items tree is unchanged because the filter and tree panels remain sibling-flat inside PageShell; lint exemption removal happens in 00260

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Every addressable on /library-releases is wrapped; reconcile inserts new rows; help bodies render; page flips to strict; lint exemption row removed
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: LibraryReleasesPage wraps the loading/error/empty/table block in <Panel name='library_releases_outstanding' title='Outstanding releases'>; PageShell return wrapped in <StrictRoute>; migration 080 pre-seeds 1 page_addressables row with source='build' (idempotent); tsc --noEmit clean; lint exemption removal happens in 00260

acceptance_criteria.criterion: lint:addressables npm script runs as a structural AST rule: any element whose first child is a heading element AND that uses border + padding tokens MUST be <Panel> or one of the audited exemption components (<InfoPanel>, <SectionHeading>); rule fails on offending file with line number
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: dev/scripts/lint_addressables.py implements PANEL_SHAPE_RE (matches <div|section className=…><h1..6>) gated by looks_like_panel() (border AND padding token); ignores nested-inside-Panel cases via 400-byte preceding-window scan; PANEL_TAGS set treats <Panel>, <InfoPanel>, <SectionHeading>, <PaneHeader> as exempt wrappers; failing matches print 'wrap in <Panel name=…>' with file:line; npm run lint:addressables wired in package.json (parallel to lint:panes)

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Pre-commit hook runs lint:addressables; CI workflow includes a separate step that runs it standalone; per-page exemption list is empty (final story removes the last entry); /dev → Reports tab shows a row labelled 'addressables' with last run timestamp + result
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: dev/git-hooks/pre-commit (symlinked to .git/hooks/pre-commit) appended a second block that runs lint_addressables.py --report and blocks commit on non-zero exit; --report writes dev/reports/<ts>-addressables.json in the same shape as paneIds reports so /dev → Reports renders it; dev/registries/addressables_exempt.json created with exempt_paths=[] (intentionally empty as a hard architectural invariant — description in the file documents this); CI workflow note: there is no .github/workflows/ in this repo, so 'CI gate' = the pre-commit hook + npm run lint:addressables script (project convention per existing lint:panes)

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Samantha SDK includes a help-manifest contract: custom apps export an optional helpDefaults: Record<kind:name_pattern, body_html> object; substrate calls RegisterFromRuntime with source='custom_app' + customAppId; library lookup runs first, then SDK manifest, then null body
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: app/contexts/SamanthaSdkContext.tsx defines SamanthaSdkContextValue {customAppId, helpDefaults}, exports <SamanthaSdkProvider customAppId helpDefaults?>, useSamanthaSdk(), and resolveSdkHelp(helpDefaults, kind, name) which matches '<kind>:<name>' exact then '<kind>:*' wildcard then null. UiAppManifest in app/store/shared/types.ts gains optional helpDefaults?: Record<string,string> with PLA-0005 contract comment documenting resolution order. app/contexts/DomRegistryContext.tsx reads useSamanthaSdk() inside useRegisterAddressable and includes {source:'custom_app', custom_app_id: sdk.customAppId} in the /api/addressables/register POST body when sdk.customAppId is set, else {source:'runtime'}; backend addressables.Service already accepts SourceCustomApp + customAppID so no backend change needed. app/components/Panel.tsx falls back to resolveSdkHelp(sdk.helpDefaults, 'panel', name) when /api/page-help/<id> returns empty body or errors. tsc --noEmit clean. RTL fixture test deferred per 00249 caveat (no frontend test framework in this repo yet).

acceptance_criteria.criterion: docs/c_c_addressables.md covers: addressing scheme + _prefix rule, six viewport slots, addressables.Service contract + write-boundary, page_help vs library_help_defaults, /dev/page-help workflow, lint:addressables rule, Samantha SDK contract, PLA-0004 closure note
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: docs/c_c_addressables.md created with all 8 sections: (1) Address scheme + leading-_ rule + NAME_RE; (2) Six viewport slots table (app|header|footer|side_bar|modal|toast); (3) Three tables (page_addressables, page_help, library_help_defaults) with constraints; (4) addressables.Service sole-writer surface + per-method caller/role table; (5) REST endpoints table including 410 Gone for deprecated /api/pane-help; (6) Frontend substrate (DomRegistryProvider, ViewportSlot, useRegisterAddressable, StrictRoute) + adopters; (7) /dev/page-help workflow; (8) lint:addressables rule with empty-exemption invariant; (9) Samantha SDK contract with helpDefaults key format + resolution order + collision rule; (10) PLA-0004 closure note enumerating what was superseded.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: .claude/CLAUDE.md gains one-line pointer to docs/c_c_addressables.md per the Authoring rule; PLA-0004 closure entry in c_scope.md notes that pane_help is superseded; PLA-0005 row added to docs/c_plan_index.md
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: .claude/CLAUDE.md gained one-line pointer 'Addressable element substrate (PLA-0005) → docs/c_c_addressables.md — …' appended after the c_c_ranking pointer per Authoring rule; docs/c_scope.md gained Underway row covering migrations 074-080 and stories 00244-00262 with explicit '**supersedes PLA-0004**' note; docs/c_plan_index.md row for PLA-0005 flipped from 'draft (awaiting review)' to 'complete', and PLA-0004 row flipped to 'complete (superseded by PLA-0005)'; PLA-0005.json date_finished set to 2026-05-02.

risks.risk: Backfill blast radius — wrapping every panel on every page risks visual regression across the whole product
risks.mitigation: Phase A proves on /dashboard alone behind a build flag; Phase B sweeps run one cluster per card with screenshot-diff verification before strict-mode flip; lenient mode default during sweep allows mixed-state pages

risks.risk: DB write-boundary leak — anyone bypassing addressables.Service to INSERT page_addressables corrupts the registry irreversibly
risks.mitigation: ripgrep test in CI fails any file outside backend/internal/addressables/ that contains 'page_addressables' in a SQL context; trust-no-one checklist updated; service has a single write transaction wrapper

risks.risk: Custom-app collision — third-party app registers a name that conflicts with a build-declared addressable
risks.mitigation: Build wins: register endpoint refuses to overwrite source='build' rows; custom-app authors must rename; collision returns 409 with the existing canonical address so the app can adapt

risks.risk: Snapshot fetch latency on first page load — every wrapped page calls /api/addressables/snapshot before any addressable mounts
risks.mitigation: Server-side cache with ETag (route + last_seen_at_max as cache key); snapshot embedded in initial HTML for SSR; client falls back to API call on cache miss; soft target < 50ms p95

risks.risk: Help-body library churn — updating a library default silently changes help text for every lazy-seeded page
risks.mitigation: Lazy seed materializes the library body into page_help on first fetch (snapshot-in-time); subsequent library updates do NOT auto-propagate; gadmin can opt-in to 're-seed from library' per row in /dev/page-help

risks.risk: Locale column unused on day one creates dead complexity
risks.mitigation: Cheap insurance — single column, default 'en', no UI; removing later costs a migration, adding later costs the same migration plus a backfill

risks.risk: Address devtool overlay leaks into production screenshots
risks.mitigation: Hard-gated NODE_ENV !== 'production' AND user.role === 'gadmin' in production; off by default; ⌥A toggle is the only entry point

risks.risk: PLA-0004 work just shipped (5 nav-prefs panels) gets re-done in this plan
risks.mitigation: PLA-0004 is the proof of concept — this plan extends it, doesn't replace it. The 5 panels get migrated forward (story 00254) not rewritten; <PaneHeader> is renamed to <Panel> in one PR

open_decisions_locked: Address scheme: samantha._viewport.<slot>._kind.name[._kind.name…] with leading underscore on every system segment (self-tokenizing).
Six viewport slots: app | header | footer | side_bar | modal | toast.
No _pageID — addresses are page-scoped by mount location; custom apps inherit address from host context.
Sibling collision rule: lint blocks duplicate (parent, kind, name) triples; runtime registry rejects with 409.
Orphan rendering: subtrees outside any <ViewportSlot> render under samantha._orphan.… with dev-console warning (visible, not crashing).
Source of truth: page_addressables Postgres table; build-time reconcile + runtime register; no page.config.ts files.
Sole writer: addressables.Service Go module; CI lint blocks direct table writes from anywhere else.
Build wins on conflict: runtime register refuses to overwrite source='build' rows.
Soft-archive on disappearance: rows stay for audit; help bodies survive panel re-add.
Dev-mode auto-register: enabled (NODE_ENV !== 'production' OR custom-app auth); prod refuses unauthenticated registers.
Custom apps as first-class: app_id partition column; same app embedded in two pages keeps one canonical sub-tree.
Help table: page_help keyed by addressable_id UUID, NOT by string paneId.
FK behaviour: ON DELETE RESTRICT + soft_archive on both sides; preserves gadmin-edited bodies if a panel is re-added.
Lazy library seed: register-time auto-seed AND read-time fallback both populate page_help; library churn does NOT silently propagate after first seed.
Locale column from day one: default 'en', no UI surface yet.
Custom-app help: hybrid — library_help_defaults first, then SDK manifest, then null body.
Adoption order: Dashboard (Phase A) → preferences → dev → portfolio → work-items+backlog → library-releases (Phase B clusters).

references.label: PLA-0004 — pane help system (parent plan; this plan extends and supersedes the architecture)
Existing pane_help table + service (migrated forward then dropped)
Trust-No-One security checklist — addressables.Service write boundary
Dev Plans tab (where this plan renders for review)
